book_claims.claim: Soy consumption modestly lowers blood pressure and improves arterial health. Isoflavones reduce osteoporosis, cancer risk and hot flush severity.
book_claims.mechanism: Soy isoflavones (genistein, daidzein) mimic oestrogen, binding to oestrogen receptors and blocking cancer cell proliferation. Soy protein replaces animal fats to reduce LDL cholesterol.

book_claims.claim: Part of cholesterol-lowering food portfolio reducing LDL-cholesterol by 13%
book_claims.mechanism: Plant protein with isoflavones that modulate LDL receptor expression and cholesterol metabolism

supplementary_research.finding: Meta-analysis (Nachvak et al., 2019) of 46 RCTs confirmed soy protein intake reduces LDL cholesterol by 3-4% and total cholesterol by 2-3%, with isoflavone-rich soy showing stronger effects than isoflavone-depleted soy protein.

Prospective study (Ma et al., Circulation 2020) of 210,000+ participants found one or more servings of tofu per week associated with 18% lower coronary heart disease risk, particularly in premenopausal women.

Systematic review (Wei et al., 2020) found soy isoflavone intake associated with 10-15% reduced risk of breast cancer recurrence in Asian populations, with equol-producing gut microbiome phenotype showing stronger protective effects.

culinary_pairings.source: Classic aromatic complement for tofu and edamame
Universal savory base for soy dishes
Traditional pairing; complementary plant protein profiles
Genistein + quercetin synergy blocks cancer cell growth at lower doses than either alone
Curcumin + genistein combined anti-inflammatory and anticancer effects

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Gut bacteria convert daidzein to equol, a more potent form. Only ~30-50% of Western populations produce equol vs. ~60% in Asian populations.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: 36 g (dried soybeans); 10 g (tofu); 18 g (1 cup edamame)
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: One of few plant proteins with all essential amino acids. Protein quality (PDCAAS) comparable to animal protein.
Particularly high in edamame; critical for homocysteine metabolism and DNA synthesis.

synergies.description: Soybeans (genistein) + quercetin-rich foods (capers, apples) block prostate and ovarian cancer cell growth at lower doses than either alone
Combined plant protein sources that together lower cholesterol through multiple mechanisms
